task_statement:
Task #9 of BRICK 6-surface audit repair goal, Global Operating Rule 9: DESIGN-ONLY decision Building (no work/implementation step -- Smith explicitly asked to run design first, review, then decide the follow-on implementation Building separately). There are currently TWO plan-materialization producers: support/operator/composition_compose.py::compose_building() (old, actually reachable from the live brick build --graph CLI route today) and support/operator/assembly.py::assemble() (intended future canonical producer per support/checkers/check_assembly_equivalence.py's own docstring: 'Guard structural equivalence for future assembly.py lowering'). This is a standing violation of Global Operating Rule 3's no-second-engine principle, tolerated only because the equivalence guard proves the two are not yet interchangeable.

THE DECISION THIS BUILDING MUST PRODUCE (not implement): (a) verify whether assemble() supports every graph_packet feature the live --graph CLI route currently exercises -- fan_in/fan_out groups, sibling_independence, closure_transition_target_policy, the graph_topology_fan_barrier checker landed 0701, the write_scope default-on-omission fix landed 0701, and any other feature compose_building() has received since the equivalence checker's docstring was written; (b) recommend ONE of two outcomes: EITHER completing the migration (CLI route switches to assemble(), compose_building retired) OR explicitly declaring compose_building permanently canonical and retiring the 'future lowering' framing on check_assembly_equivalence.py's docstring -- either resolution is acceptable per Rule 9, indefinite duplication is not; (c) if migration is recommended, produce a concrete scoped implementation plan (NOT the implementation itself) naming exact files/seams/risk; if permanent-canonical is recommended, name exactly what must change (docstring, checker framing, any dead-code retirement).

OUT OF SCOPE for this Building: any actual source mutation to composition_compose.py, assembly.py, brick/spec.py, or the CLI route itself. This Building produces a recommendation document only. The COO (Smith + Claude) will read the synthesized design plus a parallel independent research workflow's findings and declare the actual implementation Building separately.
selection_rule: Design-only Building (Smith: run design first, no work step yet) -- dual-design fan-out per the goal's Standard Phase Building Graph, stopping at design-QA/closure with no implementation lane.

nodes.work_statement: Prepare the design split. Read Global Operating Rule 9 in brick-6-surface-audit-repair-goal-0630.md, the write_scope finding doc (brick-6-graph-route-missing-default-write-scope-finding-0701.md), and check_assembly_equivalence.py's module docstring in full. Return an invariant map and dispatch notes for the two independent design lanes. Do not edit files. Do not implement anything.

Codex design lane: CODE-SURFACE feature-parity investigation. Read support/operator/assembly.py::assemble() and support/operator/composition_compose.py::compose_building() in full, side by side. Produce an exact feature checklist: for each of fan_in groups, fan_out groups, sibling_independence, closure_transition_target_policy, the graph_topology_fan_barrier checker (landed 0701, commit 2405a9d), and the write_scope default-on-omission fix (landed 0701, commit 6c3c73e) -- does assemble() support it, does compose_building() support it, and if only one does, exact file:line evidence. Also check for any feature compose_building() lacks that assemble() has (assembly.py's write_scope default was the original finding; check for others). Estimate migration cost if the CLI route switched to assemble() (call sites to change, risk surface). Return design/investigation only, no source edits.

Claude design lane: ARCHITECTURE and risk framing, independent of the Codex code-surface lane. Address: is this genuinely a Rule-3 (no-second-engine) violation or an acceptable transitional state; what is the risk/cost of completing the assemble() migration (customer route stability, blast radius) versus the risk/cost of declaring compose_building permanently canonical (indefinite duplication, is this actually sustainable); does either outcome change Brick/Agent/Link axis meaning (it must not); what would 'explicitly declaring compose_building permanently canonical' concretely require in check_assembly_equivalence.py's docstring and framing. Recommend ONE of the two outcomes with reasoning, and mark genuinely undecidable points as not_proven rather than smoothing them. Return design only, no source edits.

Synthesize the Codex code-surface lane and the Claude architecture/risk lane into ONE recommendation document: confirmed feature-parity table (with citations), the recommended outcome (migrate vs permanent-canonical) with reasoning, and if migrate is recommended a concrete scoped implementation plan (files/seams/risk, not the implementation itself). This is closure-synthesis of design evidence only, not success/quality judgment, not an implementation. If the two lanes disagree or evidence is insufficient to recommend either outcome, return Link-facing transition_concern_evidence targeting the intake-split node rather than forcing a recommendation.

Gemini-local design QA. Attack the synthesized recommendation before it reaches the COO. Verify: is the feature-parity table actually backed by file:line citations (not asserted); does the recommendation change Brick/Agent/Link axis meaning (it must not); is 'either resolution acceptable' honestly represented rather than the QA lane injecting its own preference; are unresolved points honestly in not_proven rather than smoothed. Do not source-mutate. Return local observations and null/non-reroute concern if the recommendation is well-supported, or targeted concern evidence if not.

Final closure of this DESIGN-ONLY Building. Report the synthesized recommendation, the design-QA verdict, observed_evidence / narrowly_proven / not_proven / next Movement candidate. This Building's scope ends here -- do NOT attempt implementation, do NOT claim task #9 itself is closed (only the design/recommendation phase is). If design-QA raised an unresolved concern, HOLD rather than smoothing it.

proof_limits: official brick build graph evidence is support evidence only
this Building is design/recommendation only, it does not implement or close task #9 itself
not source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority
not_proven: the actual implementation of whichever outcome is recommended (separate follow-on Building)
P8 ship safety, P9 dynamic customer replay, fresh-machine install, real-provider reliability, customer comprehension
